_doc: Signature-decoration registry (composition-DNA lever #3) — the palette of decorative DEVICES a theme can declare as its signature, cataloged from the 34-template study (docs/REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_ANALYSIS.md, the ~10 motif groups G1-G10). A device is a named, positionable, token-driven primitive. A theme.json declares `decoration: [<device-id>, ...]`; compose.py's _theme_decorations renders the declared devices as a canvas-wide overlay in the theme's own tokens. `kind`: shared = a reusable parameterized primitive (same executor, theme palette varies it); signature = an identity-defining device. `scope`: frame = canvas-wide (always-on furniture); scene = tied to a scene's content (not yet wired). Every device here MUST have a renderer in compose._DECOR_RENDERERS (honesty-tested), and every theme's declared devices must be ids in here.

graph-paper.purpose: a ruled graph-paper grid wash — engineering / technical / trend-index register (blueprint, cobalt-grid).

scanlines.purpose: CRT / terminal scanline overlay — retro-digital / phosphor register (terminal-green, 8-bit, retro-windows).

grain.purpose: fractal print grain — paper / riso / photographic register; the single most common signature texture. Near-universal in the reference decks.

dot-grid.purpose: a faint dot-grid wash — quiet structured paper (block-frame, blue-professional).

corner-brackets.purpose: L-shaped marks at the four corners that imply a frame without enclosing it (8-bit-orbit, block-frame).

interior-frame.purpose: a hairline border inset ~4% — a poster / placard 'design within a design' (pink-script, peoples-platform, signal).

rail-label.purpose: a vertical rotated wayfinder label along the left edge — magazine-spine metadata (biennale-yellow, cobalt-grid).

params.text: the label string (default a generic marker)
